text: I went to hospital today because I have had pain in my right leg for a week.
correct: I went to hospital today because I had had pain in my right leg for a week. Using tenses correctly is one of the most difficult things in English. You go to hospital at some point in the past. You have the pain before that point in the past. So "I went to hospital today" is ok - it's past tense. But it should be "I had had pain", because that happened before that point in the past. So you should use past perfect which is "past before past" tense.
